lints.message: Escalation clause (Proposal §4.3) has no cumulative cap.
lints.explanation: This clause allows fees to increase every period with no ceiling. Over a multi-year term this compounds into a large, unbounded cost. Market-standard contracts cap cumulative escalation (commonly 5-10%).

lints.message: No proration policy found for usage-based pricing.
lints.explanation: The contract prices per-unit or tiered usage but never states how mid-cycle changes (a seat added on day 15) are billed. Vendors will default to whatever favors them -- usually full-period billing with no proration. Silence here is inherently a vendor-favorable term.

Auto-renewing contracts with a short opt-out window routinely trap customers into another full term (often with an uplift) because the cancellation deadline passes unnoticed. Market standard is a 60-90 day notice window.

lints.message: Escalation clause (Proposal §4.3) fires quarterly, not annually.
lints.explanation: Quarterly escalation is far more aggressive than the market-standard annual cadence -- it compounds 4x faster over the same period.

summary: TalentBridge HR Suite's contract carries 2 high-severity issue(s), most notably: Escalation clause (Proposal §4.3) has no cumulative cap. Risk score: 90/100.

results.name: Escalation frequency must be annual, not quarterly
